meta.source_paper: Address poisoning attacks (SlowMist reports), EIP-155 chain ID specification

risk_domain.name: Wrong Chain / Address Prevention
risk_domain.description: Pre-submission validation to prevent irreversible loss from sending to wrong chain, wrong address, poisoned address, or non-functional contract. Wallet guard that catches common transfer mistakes.

capabilities: address poisoning via dust transactions with similar-looking addresses
monitoring user transaction history to craft targeted poisoning attacks
deploying decoy contracts that mimic legitimate addresses on wrong chains
limitations: cannot modify user's address book or wallet UI
cannot intercept transactions after private relay submission

H1_no_activity_on_target_chain.name: Address has no activity on target chain
H1_no_activity_on_target_chain.description: Recipient address has zero transaction history on the target chain but is active on other chains. This suggests the user may be sending to the right address on the wrong network, resulting in inaccessible funds.

signals.name: no_target_chain_history
signals.description: Address has zero transactions (sent or received) on the target chain

signals.name: active_on_other_chains
signals.description: Same address has transaction history on at least one other EVM chain

signals.name: user_history_different_chain
signals.description: User has previously sent to this address on a different chain

recommendations.description: Confirm the intended chain with the recipient. Address exists on other networks but not this one.
Send a minimal test amount first to verify the address is reachable on target chain

H1_no_activity_on_target_chain.fundamental_limitation: EVM address reuse across chains means any valid address can technically receive on any chain, even if the recipient cannot access funds there. The check cannot distinguish deliberate cross-chain sends from mistakes.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Set of N transfers where some target correct chain and others target wrong chain
benchmark_scenario.metric: fraction of wrong-chain transfers correctly flagged with acceptable false positive rate
benchmark_scenario.baseline: estimated 2-5% of user transfers go to addresses with no target-chain activity

H2_contract_cannot_receive.name: Contract address cannot receive tokens
H2_contract_cannot_receive.description: Recipient is a smart contract that lacks a receive/fallback function for ETH or does not implement the required token receiver interface. Tokens sent to such contracts are permanently locked.

signals.name: no_receive_function
signals.description: Contract bytecode has no receive() or fallback() function for ETH transfers

signals.name: no_token_receiver
signals.description: Contract does not implement ERC-721/1155 receiver interface for NFT transfers

detection.threshold: no_receive_function for ETH or no_token_receiver for NFTs triggers BLOCK

recommendations.description: Prevent transaction -- tokens would be permanently locked in the contract
If the contract has a deposit function, suggest using that instead of direct transfer

H2_contract_cannot_receive.fundamental_limitation: Bytecode analysis can miss proxy contracts where the implementation is upgradeable. A contract that can receive today may not be able to tomorrow, and vice versa.

benchmark_scenario.setup: N transfers to contracts with and without receive/fallback, including proxy patterns
benchmark_scenario.metric: detection rate of non-receivable contracts with false positive rate on proxies
benchmark_scenario.baseline: millions of dollars lost annually to tokens sent to non-receivable contracts

H3_address_poisoning.name: Address poisoning
H3_address_poisoning.description: A similar-looking address appears in the user's transaction history, planted by a dust attacker. Users who copy addresses from history may copy the attacker's address instead of the intended recipient.

signals.name: similar_address_in_history
signals.description: Recipient matches first 4 and last 4 characters of a different address the user previously interacted with

signals.name: recent_dust_from_similar
signals.description: User received a dust transaction (<$0.01) from an address visually similar to the recipient

signals.name: address_age_mismatch
signals.description: Recipient address was created very recently (< 7 days) and has only dust transactions

recommendations.description: Block transaction and show the legitimate address alongside the poisoned one for comparison
Always use saved address book entries instead of copying from transaction history

H3_address_poisoning.fundamental_limitation: Address poisoning exploits UI patterns, not protocol flaws. As long as wallets display truncated addresses and users copy from history, this attack vector persists.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Inject N poisoned addresses into simulated transaction history, measure detection
benchmark_scenario.metric: fraction of poisoned addresses correctly identified before user sends
benchmark_scenario.baseline: address poisoning caused >$40M in losses in 2023 (SlowMist)

H4_chain_id_mismatch.name: Chain ID mismatch
H4_chain_id_mismatch.description: Transaction is being signed with a chain ID that doesn't match the intended target network. This can result in the transaction being replayed on the wrong chain or failing entirely.

signals.name: chain_id_wrong
signals.description: Transaction chain ID does not match the RPC endpoint's reported chain ID

signals.name: network_name_mismatch
signals.description: User-selected network name resolves to a different chain ID than expected

recommendations.description: Prevent transaction from being broadcast until chain ID is corrected
Switch wallet to the correct network matching the intended recipient chain

H4_chain_id_mismatch.fundamental_limitation: EIP-155 replay protection prevents cross-chain replay but doesn't prevent a user from intentionally signing for the wrong chain. Detection relies on inferring user intent.

benchmark_scenario.setup: N transactions with correct and incorrect chain IDs across mainnet, L2s, and testnets
benchmark_scenario.metric: detection rate of mismatched chain IDs with zero false positives
benchmark_scenario.baseline: chain ID mismatches are deterministic -- 100% detection expected

H5_deprecated_contract.name: Deprecated contract
H5_deprecated_contract.description: Recipient contract is paused, has been migrated to a new version, or appears abandoned (no transactions in 90+ days). Sending funds may result in locked tokens or interaction with outdated logic.

signals.name: contract_paused
signals.description: Contract has a paused state variable set to true or circuit breaker is active

signals.name: migration_detected
signals.description: Contract emitted a migration event or has a successor address registered

signals.name: abandoned_contract
signals.description: Contract has had no transactions in 90+ days and no governance activity

recommendations.description: Look up the new contract address and redirect the transaction to the migrated version
Display warning about deprecated contract status and let user decide

H5_deprecated_contract.fundamental_limitation: Not all abandoned contracts are deprecated -- some are intentionally immutable. Contract pause detection depends on standard patterns; custom implementations may not be detectable.

benchmark_scenario.setup: N contracts including active, paused, migrated, and abandoned, with known ground truth
benchmark_scenario.metric: accuracy in classifying contract status across all categories
benchmark_scenario.baseline: paused/migrated detection near 100% for standard patterns, lower for custom

address_validator.description: Compare recipient address against known contacts, detect visual similarity with poisoned addresses

chain_checker.description: Check address activity across EVM chains to detect wrong-chain sends

contract_status_checker.description: Check contract pause status, migration history, and activity recency

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring detection of wrong-chain and wrong-address transfers before submission

methodology.step_1: Generate N test transfers: correct, wrong-chain, poisoned-address, non-receivable contract, chain-ID mismatch
methodology.step_2: Run all 5 heuristics on each transfer
methodology.step_4: Measure false positive rate on legitimate transfers
methodology.step_5: Estimate funds saved by blocked transactions

combined_benchmark.target: detect >95% of wrong-chain/wrong-address transfers with <5% false positive rate

adversary_levels.name: accidental
adversary_levels.description: User mistakes -- wrong chain, non-receivable contract

templates.skill_suggestion: To prevent {heuristic_name}, I can {skill_action}. This catches ~{effectiveness_pct}% of cases. {user_cost_note}
